name: Saffron-Infused Cloud Soufflé Pancake
description: A molecular approach to brunch featuring vacuum-compressed berries, saffron-infused foam, and hyper-aerated soufflé structures.

steps.instruction: Prepare the Compressed Berries and Saffron Foam.
Bloom the saffron threads in 10ml of warm milk for 15 minutes.
In a copper bowl, whisk egg yolks with the saffron milk and vanilla seeds until pale and ribbon-like.
Gently fold the triple-sifted cake flour into the yolk mixture.
In a separate ultra-clean bowl, whisk chilled egg whites and castor sugar to a 'stiff-bird-beak' peak.
Using a silhouette motion, fold the whites into the yolk base in three stages to maintain maximum aeration.
Set the induction cooktop to exactly 165°C.
Place non-stick ring molds on the surface and pipe the batter until 3/4 full.
Add 5ml of distilled water to the pan and cover immediately with a heavy lid to create steam convection.
Cook for 6 minutes, flip with the offset spatula, and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
Prepare the Manuka Honey Gastrique.
Plating: Stack three pancakes in the center of a chilled stone plate.
Discharge a perfect quenelle of saffron foam from the iSi Whip onto the crown.
Arrange compressed berries and dehydrated honeycomb around the base; finish with 24k gold leaf.

winePairings.name: Chateau d'Yquem Sauternes
winePairings.notes: Honeyed sweetness and apricot notes mirror the Manuka gastrique.

winePairings.name: Moët & Chandon Grand Vintage Rose
winePairings.notes: The acidity cuts through the creamy saffron foam and rich egg yolks.

steps.instruction: Place berries and syrup in a vacuum bag.
Seal in a Chamber Vacuum Sealer at maximum pressure to alter cell structure.
Chill for 20 minutes before serving.
In a copper saucier, combine honey and vinegar.
Reduce over medium heat until it achieves a thick 'nappe' consistency.
Mix ingredients and pour into Gourmet Whip.
Charge with two N2O cartridges and shake vigorously.
Hold in a 4°C water bath.
